ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Maggi instant noodle soup; closest parent 'noodle soup food product' (FOODON_00002473) used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC (Soups and broths) is the closest defensible parent category for instant noodle soup; no specific Maggi soup code exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept identified for instant noodle soup as a prepared dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of Maggi soup is approximately 350 g, comprising one block of instant noodles (about 70 g dry weight) reconstituted in approximately 280 ml of water with the seasoning sachet.

nutrient_source.title: Instant noodles, dry, cooked — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy; supplemented with Nestlé Maggi 2-Minute Noodles product label data (USDA Branded Foods) for sodium and seasoning contribution
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service / Nestlé product data via USDA Branded Foods
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for cooked instant noodles (FDC ID 172030) and cross-referenced with USDA Branded Foods entries for Maggi instant noodle products to account for seasoning sachet contribution, particularly sodium. Values represent the prepared soup (noodles plus broth) per 100 g. Sodium is elevated due to the seasoning sachet. IMR Malaysia MyFCD does not have a specific Maggi soup entry; USDA data is the closest approved source.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Instant noodles (cooked)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI of 52 is based on the University of Sydney GI Database value for cooked instant noodles (wheat-based), which is the closest approved analogous food to prepared Maggi soup noodles. Instant wheat noodles have a reported GI of approximately 47–52 in peer-reviewed literature (Atkinson et al., 2021, AJCN). A value of 52 is used as a conservative upper estimate. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (52 × 35.7) / 100 ≈ 18.6, where carbohydrate per 350 g serving = 10.2 × 3.5 = 35.7 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Very high sodium content from seasoning sachet (approximately 420 mg sodium per 100 g; ~1470 mg per full serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid Maggi soup due to its very high sodium content from the seasoning sachet, which can contribute more than 60% of the recommended daily sodium limit (2000 mg) in a single serving; reducing or omitting the seasoning sachet is strongly advised.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index (GI 52) and moderate glycemic load per serving (~18.6) from refined wheat noodles
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes can consume Maggi soup in controlled portions, as the GI of 52 is in the low range; however, the glycemic load per standard serving is moderate (~18.6), so portion control and pairing with protein or vegetables is recommended to blunt postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High sodium content and presence of saturated fat and palm oil in the seasoning and noodle block
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit Maggi soup consumption due to its high sodium content (~1470 mg per serving) and saturated fat from palm oil, both of which are associated with elevated blood pressure and adverse lipid profiles; occasional consumption with reduced seasoning and added vegetables may be acceptable.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Maggi Soup (also spelled Maggie Soup) is a popular instant noodle soup dish widely consumed in Malaysia, Singapore, and across Southeast Asia. It is prepared by boiling a block of Maggi brand instant wheat noodles and dissolving the accompanying seasoning powder sachet in hot water to create a flavourful broth. The dish is characterised by its soft, slightly chewy noodle texture and a savoury, umami-rich broth with notes of chicken, onion, and MSG. It is an ultra-processed convenience food that can be prepared in under five minutes. In Malaysia and Singapore, Maggi soup is commonly served as a late-night snack, budget meal, or comfort food, and is often enhanced with a raw or soft-boiled egg, sliced chilli, spring onions, or canned sardines. Nutritionally, a standard 350 g serving provides approximately 252 kcal, 8.4 g protein, 8.75 g fat, and 35.7 g carbohydrate. The sodium content is very high at approximately 1470 mg per serving due to the seasoning sachet, making it a concern for individuals with hypertension and cardiovascular disease. The glycemic index of the wheat noodle component is approximately 52 (low GI), but the glycemic load per serving is moderate at ~18.6. The dish is halal-certified in most commercial formulations. It is not suitable for individuals on low-sodium diets and should be consumed in moderation by those managing blood pressure, cardiovascular risk, or blood glucose levels.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Maggi soup?
Is Maggi soup high in sodium?
What is the GI of instant noodles?
Is Maggie soup the same as Maggi soup?
How much sodium in a bowl of Maggi soup?
Can I eat Maggi soup if I have high blood pressure?

confidence_reason: No exact Maggi soup entry exists in IMR Malaysia MyFCD or HPB Singapore Foodo. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for cooked instant noodles and cross-referenced with USDA Branded Foods entries for Maggi products. GI is estimated from the closest analogous approved source (University of Sydney GI Database, instant wheat noodles). Confidence is medium because the values are well-supported by approved secondary sources but are not an exact match for the specific restaurant preparation at New Shah Alam, Circular Road.
notes: 1. Nutrient values represent the prepared soup (noodles plus broth) per 100 g, adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 172030, cooked instant noodles) with sodium adjusted upward to reflect the full seasoning sachet contribution, consistent with USDA Branded Foods Maggi product data. 2. Vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy entry for cooked instant noodles. 3. Vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source for this preparation. 4. Vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as instant noodle soup contains no animal-derived B12 source and the value is not reported in the source. 5. Folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source. 6. Vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the source database for this food. 7. Vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source. 8. Selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the primary source. 9. Trans_fat_g is 0.0 — confirmed zero per USDA Branded Foods Maggi product data; modern Maggi formulations use palm oil without hydrogenation. 10. Cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero as the base product contains no animal-derived cholesterol; restaurant additions (egg, meat) are not included in this base record. 11. Vitamin_c_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero as instant noodle soup contains no vitamin C-bearing ingredients in the base formulation. 12. GI of 52 is an analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database data for cooked instant wheat noodles; no direct GI measurement for Maggi soup as a complete dish was found in approved sources. 13. The cuisine_tags in the input included 'Dosa', which is inconsistent with this dish and has been disregarded. 14. The food_name in the input ('Maggie Soup') uses an alternate spelling; the canonical English name 'Maggi Soup (Instant Noodle Soup)' is used in this record. 15. Serving size of 350 g is a standard estimate for one bowl of prepared Maggi soup.
